perturbation.original_text: The Board, based on the information provided to it, has determined that each member of the 2020 Director Slate would (i) qualify as an "independent director" under the applicable rules of the New York Stock Exchange (the "NYSE") and the rules and regulations of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and (ii) satisfy the guidelines and policies with respect to service on the Board applicable to all non- management directors (other than Mr. Falcone).
perturbation.changed_text: The Board, based on the information provided to it, has determined that each member of the 2020 Director Slate would satisfy the guidelines and policies with respect to service on the Board applicable to all non- management directors (other than Mr. Falcone).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that 'each member of the 2020 Director Slate would (i) qualify as an "independent director" under the applicable rules of the New York Stock Exchange (the "NYSE") and the rules and regulations of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC")', introduces the possibility that the appointed directors, might not qualify as independent directors under SEC and NYSE rules. This could be a breach of fiduciary duty if elsewhere in the document there are requirements to have independent board members or the company presented itself to be abiding by SEC regulations in good standing. The in-text contradiction arises in the 2020 Proxy Statement because the company states that the board is formed with independent directors
perturbation.contradicted_text: HC2 HOLDINGS AND MG CAPITAL ANNOUNCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND PLAN TO RECONSTITUTE BOARD OF DIRECTORS Announces Immediate Appointment of Two New Directors: MG Capital Nominees Kenneth S. Courtis and Michael Gorzynski Previously Announced Additions Avram A. "Avie" Glazer and Shelly C. Lombard Will Also Begin Serving as Directors Immediately, With Mr. Glazer to Serve as Chairman of the Board Recent Collaboration With Stockholders Will Result in More Than 50% of the Board Being Refreshed Following the 2020 Annual Meeting MG Capital Agrees to Withdraw its Consent Solicitation and Nomination Notice
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the director slate qualifies as "independent directors" under NYSE and SEC rules. The changed text removes this explicit confirmation, creating uncertainty about their independence. The contradicted text announces the plan to reconstitute the board of directors, implying that the board will be formed with independent directors. By removing the explicit assurance of independence, the changed text contradicts the spirit and intent of the announcement, which relies on the assumption of director independence for proper board function and compliance. The removal causes a direct conflict with the implied understanding of independence in the announcement.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, provided that each member of the 2020 Director Slate is able and willing to serve on the Board, (i) the Board will unanimously recommend that the stockholders of the Company vote to elect each member of the 2020 Director Slate as a director of the Company at the 2020 Annual Meeting, (ii) the Company will use its reasonable best efforts (which will include the solicitation of proxies) to obtain the election of the 2020 Director Slate at the 2020 Annual Meeting and (iii) the Company will use its best efforts to resist, discourage and defend against any attempt, solicitation or action by any stockholder of the Company to remove any member of the 2020 Director Slate prior to the 2021 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, (i) the Board will unanimously recommend that the stockholders of the Company vote to elect each member of the 2020 Director Slate as a director of the Company at the 2020 Annual Meeting, (ii) the Company will use its reasonable best efforts (which will include the solicitation of proxies) to obtain the election of the 2020 Director Slate at the 2020 Annual Meeting and (iii) the Company will use its best efforts to resist, discourage and defend against any attempt, solicitation or action by any stockholder of the Company to remove any member of the 2020 Director Slate prior to the 2021 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided that each member of the 2020 Director Slate is able and willing to serve on the Board', the company is now obligated to push forward with the election even if a director is unable or unwilling to serve. This creates an in-text contradiction, with Section 1d ('New Director Information. As a condition to the New Directors' appointment to the Board and any subsequent nomination for election as a director at any future Company annual meeting of stockholders, he or she must provide any information required to be disclosed in a proxy statement or other filing under applicable law, stock exchange rules or listing standards.'), which implies each director has to actively fulfill conditions to be appointed. This could cause enforcement issues because the company has to pursue the election of a director that may not fulfill the company policies
perturbation.contradicted_text: New Director Information. As a condition to the New Directors' appointment to the Board and any subsequent nomination for election as a director at any future Company annual meeting of stockholders, he or she must provide any information required to be disclosed in a proxy statement or other filing under applicable law, stock exchange rules or listing standards.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition that each member of the 2020 Director Slate must be 'able and willing to serve on the Board'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that 'As a condition to the New Directors' appointment to the Board...he or she must provide any information required to be disclosed...'. By removing the condition, the company is obligated to push for the election of a director even if they don't fulfill the requirements, leading to a direct conflict between the two clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to applicable law, each of the Parties covenants and agrees that, during the Standstill Period, neither Party nor any of its subsidiaries, Affiliates, successors, assigns, principals, partners, members, general partners, officers, key employees or directors (collectively, "Representatives"), shall in any way, directly or indirectly, in any capacity or manner, whether written or oral, electronically or otherwise (including, without limitation, in a television, radio, internet, newspaper, magazine interview, or otherwise through the press, media, analysts or other persons or in any document or report filed with the SEC), publicly disparage, impugn, make ad hominem attacks on or otherwise defame or slander or make, express, transmit, speak, write, verbalize or otherwise publicly communicate in any way (or cause, further, assist, solicit, encourage, support or participate in any of the foregoing), any public communication or statement of any kind, whether verbal, in writing, electronically transferred or otherwise, that might reasonably be construed to be disparage, derogate or impugn, the other Party or such other Party's Representatives (including any current officer or director of a Party or a Parties' subsidiaries who no longer serves in such capacity following the execution of this Agreement), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), or any of their businesses, products or services, in any manner that would reasonably be expected to damage the business, or reputation of the other Party or of its Representatives (including former officers and directors), directors (or former directors), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party); provided that, with respect to any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding between the Parties, nothing in this Section 5 shall prevent either Party from disclosing any facts or circumstances with respect to any such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties covenants and agrees that, during the Standstill Period, neither Party nor any of its subsidiaries, Affiliates, successors, assigns, principals, partners, members, general partners, officers, key employees or directors (collectively, "Representatives"), shall in any way, directly or indirectly, in any capacity or manner, whether written or oral, electronically or otherwise (including, without limitation, in a television, radio, internet, newspaper, magazine interview, or otherwise through the press, media, analysts or other persons or in any document or report filed with the SEC), publicly disparage, impugn, make ad hominem attacks on or otherwise defame or slander or make, express, transmit, speak, write, verbalize or otherwise publicly communicate in any way (or cause, further, assist, solicit, encourage, support or participate in any of the foregoing), any public communication or statement of any kind, whether verbal, in writing, electronically transferred or otherwise, that might reasonably be construed to be disparage, derogate or impugn, the other Party or such other Party's Representatives (including any current officer or director of a Party or a Parties' subsidiaries who no longer serves in such capacity following the execution of this Agreement), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), or any of their businesses, products or services, in any manner that would reasonably be expected to damage the business, or reputation of the other Party or of its Representatives (including former officers and directors), directors (or former directors), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party); provided that, with respect to any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding between the Parties, nothing in this Section 5 shall prevent either Party from disclosing any facts or circumstances with respect to any such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'Subject to applicable law' from the non-disparagement clause creates an internal contradiction. This is because in the section, 6(i) now the party can't act with his or her fiduciary duties or otherwise in accordance with applicable law, that allows directors to fulfill their fiduciary duties under applicable law. If a director's fiduciary duty requires them to disclose information that could be construed as disparaging, this clause would prevent them from doing so, violating their duty. Also in the previous text it says that the this section, (i) limit the power of any director of the Company to act in accordance with his or her fiduciary duties or otherwise in accordance with applicable law and (ii) limit any Party's ability to comply with any subpoena or other legal process or respond to a request for information from any governmental authority with jurisdiction over the party from whom information is sought.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Section 5 shall not (i) limit the power of any director of the Company to act in accordance with his or her fiduciary duties or otherwise in accordance with applicable law and (ii) limit any Party's ability to comply with any subpoena or other legal process or respond to a request for information from any governmental authority with jurisdiction over the party from whom information is sought.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'Subject to applicable law' from the non-disparagement clause directly contradicts the clause stating that Section 5 shall not limit a director's power to act in accordance with their fiduciary duties or compliance with legal processes. By removing the qualifier, the modified text creates a scenario where the non-disparagement clause could prevent a director from fulfilling their legal obligations, thus creating a conflict between the changed text and the contradicted text.
